title: Climate Research Fellowship 2026: Fully-Funded Opportunity to Become a Global Climate Leader
summary: The Climate Research Fellowship 2026 is a transformative opportunity designed for passionate individuals who are ready to drive meaningful environmental change through research, advocacy, and innovation. This prestigious fellowship unites globally recognized climate organizations to nurture and empower the next generation of climate leaders. If you are deeply committed to tackling climate challenges and want to contribute to impactful research while gaining international exposure, this fellowship provides the perfect platform to elevate your journey. About the Climate Research Fellowship 2026 The fellowship is a collaborative initiative led by leading environmental and academic institutions, including: BRAC Climate Change Programme Centre for Climate Change and Environmental Research (C3ER) BRAC University Center for Atmospheric Pollution Studies (CAPS) Mission Green Bangladesh (MGB) Together, these organizations aim to cultivate innovative thinkers and changemakers who can address pressing climate issues through research-driven solutions. Why You Should Apply This fellowship is not just a learning program—it is a comprehensive career-building experience designed to equip participants with the tools, exposure, and networks needed to succeed in the climate sector. Key Benefits Four Months of Intensive Training Structured modules covering climate science, policy, and research methodologies Delivered by top academic and industry experts Fully-Funded International Opportunity Top 5 fellows will receive an all-expenses-paid trip to Nepal Participate in the 2nd International Climate Camp 2026 (July 7th–11th) Expert Mentorship Direct guidance from national and international environmental leaders Personalized support to refine your research and advocacy work Research Development Support Access to relevant data and field resources Workshops on academic writing, policy engagement, and research presentation Global Visibility Opportunities to publish research findings Media exposure at national and international levels Core Research Areas Fellows will focus on critical climate challenges that demand urgent attention: Climate-induced displacement and migration Urban heat stress and green adaptation strategies Climate finance and community resilience Gender-responsive environmental leadership Renewable energy access and sustainable development Roles and Responsibilities of Fellows Participants in the fellowship are expected to actively contribute to research and advocacy efforts through: Investigation Conduct research addressing real-world environmental problems Develop innovative and practical climate solutions Advocacy Engage communities and policymakers Organize awareness campaigns and outreach initiatives Content Creation Produce impactful communication materials Inspire action through storytelling and research dissemination Program Contribution Assist in organizing the International Climate Camp 2026 Collaborate with fellow participants and mentors Program Structure and Logistics Duration: 4 months Commitment: 3 days per week (approximately 4–8 hours daily) Format: Hybrid (online and in-person engagement where applicable) Stipend: Non-stipendiary (focus on professional development and international exposure) Application Process Required Documents Applicants must prepare and submit the following: Curriculum Vitae (CV) Maximum 2 pages Statement of Purpose (SoP) Maximum 300 words Explain your motivation and interest Research Proposal Maximum 1 page Outline your proposed study Writing Sample Previous research or environmental writing (published or unpublished) Application Fee 200 BDT (non-refundable) Payment via bKash Important Note: If you have already paid for the International Climate Camp 2026 and are selected as a fellow, your payment will be refunded. APPLY HERE Applicants must upload all required documents in a Google Drive folder and provide the link in the application form. Deadline and Contact Information Application Deadline: May 15, 2026 Email: climateresearchfellowship@gmail.com Phone: +8801733806880 Final Thoughts The Climate Research Fellowship 2026 is more than just an academic program—it is a launchpad for individuals who aspire to lead climate action on a global scale. With access to mentorship, international exposure, and impactful research opportunities, fellows will be positioned at the forefront of environmental change. If you are ready to transform your passion into measurable impact, this fellowship is your chance to step forward and make a difference. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here . title: Executive Director (Remote) at State Democracy Project: High-Level Nonprofit Leadership Opportunity in the U.S.
summary: The State Democracy Project is currently hiring a full-time Executive Director to lead its mission of strengthening democratic participation and supporting grassroots initiatives across the United States. This senior leadership opportunity is ideal for experienced nonprofit professionals who are passionate about social justice, civic engagement, and empowering historically marginalized communities. With a fully remote structure and a competitive salary package, this role offers both flexibility and significant national impact. About State Democracy Project (SDP) State Democracy Project (SDP) is a nonprofit organization focused on improving democracy in the United States by increasing citizen participation and fostering more responsive government institutions at the local, state, and federal levels. A key feature of SDP’s work is its fiscal sponsorship model , which enables grassroots initiatives—especially those led by underrepresented communities—to operate effectively without the administrative burden of establishing independent nonprofit structures. SDP is committed to: Supporting community-driven projects Promoting civic education and leadership Maintaining low sponsorship fees for accessibility Strengthening democratic systems through collaboration The Opportunity SDP is seeking a strategic and experienced Executive Director to guide the organization into its next phase of growth and impact. This is a unique opportunity to: Lead a mission-driven organization Work closely with grassroots leaders and funders Shape the future of democratic engagement initiatives Manage a small but highly impactful team The Executive Director will play a central role in expanding SDP’s reach, strengthening partnerships, and ensuring operational excellence. Position Overview Reporting directly to the Board of Directors, the Executive Director is responsible for: Overseeing daily operations Driving long-term strategic growth Ensuring legal and financial compliance Managing partnerships and external relations Representing the organization publicly The role combines strategic leadership with hands-on management , requiring both vision and operational expertise. Key Responsibilities Organizational Leadership Lead all internal operations, including programs, finance, and administration Build and strengthen organizational infrastructure Ensure transparency, accountability, and integrity in all activities Strategic Direction Develop and implement long-term strategies Partner with the Board on decision-making and goal setting Navigate complex political and nonprofit environments Partnerships and Outreach Build relationships with funders, donors, and stakeholders Expand SDP’s network of sponsored projects Represent the organization as its primary spokesperson Program and Project Support Provide guidance to sponsored projects on fundraising and development Ensure high-quality service delivery and compliance Monitor project performance and impact Team Leadership Lead, coach, and support staff and consultants Foster a culture of collaboration, respect, and accountability Candidate Profile The ideal candidate is a seasoned nonprofit leader with strong strategic and operational capabilities. Core Qualifications Minimum 10 years of senior leadership experience in nonprofit organizations Strong understanding of fiscal sponsorship models Experience working with Boards of Directors Proven ability to manage complex organizations and environments Demonstrated commitment to social justice and marginalized communities Additional Skills Excellent communication and public speaking abilities Experience in fundraising and donor engagement Strong risk management and compliance knowledge Ability to build and maintain partnerships Adaptability and leadership in uncertain environments Salary and Benefits Salary Range: $130,000 – $150,000 annually Benefits Include: Generous paid time off (PTO) Comprehensive health benefits 401(k) retirement plan Work Location Fully remote position (United States-based organization) Application Process To apply, candidates must submit: A detailed resume A concise and compelling cover letter explaining: Interest in the role Relevant experience and qualifications Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis , so early submission is highly recommended. Equal Opportunity Statement State Democracy Project is committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace and does not discriminate based on race, gender, religion, nationality, disability, or other protected characteristics. For more information click here APPLY HERE Discover more opportunity here The post Executive Director (Remote) at State Democracy Project: High-Level Nonprofit Leadership Opportunity in the U.S. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Canada’s $23.8M Digital Skills for Youth Program 2026
summary: The Government of Canada has officially launched a major funding opportunity under the Digital Skills for Youth (DS4Y) Program , investing $23.8 million to help young people develop critical skills for the fast-evolving digital economy. Announced by Mélanie Joly , this initiative is part of a broader national effort to strengthen Canada’s workforce and prepare youth for careers in emerging technological fields. This call for proposals represents a significant opportunity for organizations to secure funding while contributing to youth employment, innovation, and digital transformation across the country. About the Digital Skills for Youth (DS4Y) Program The Digital Skills for Youth (DS4Y) program is a flagship initiative under the Youth Employment and Skills Strategy (YESS) , led by Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada . The program focuses on equipping young Canadians with: Digital and technical skills Hands-on work experience Access to employment opportunities in high-demand sectors Since its launch in 2018, DS4Y has already supported over 6,900 youth internships , making it a proven and impactful initiative. Purpose of the 2026 Call for Proposals Through this new funding round, the Government of Canada is seeking qualified organizations to deliver training and employment opportunities for youth. The key objective is to: Build a future-ready workforce Help youth adapt to technological advancements Strengthen Canada’s digital economy Selected organizations will play a crucial role in connecting young people with employers and equipping them with practical, job-ready skills. Funding Details Total Funding: $23.8 million Funding Period: 2026–2027 to 2027–2028 Program Type: Government-funded initiative (via call for proposals) This funding will be distributed to organizations capable of implementing impactful training and employment programs for youth. Focus Areas and Skills Development The DS4Y program emphasizes training in high-growth and future-oriented sectors, including: Cybersecurity Artificial Intelligence (AI) Big Data and data analytics Automation of knowledge-based tasks Digital innovation and technology-driven industries These areas reflect the skills most in demand in today’s and tomorrow’s labor market. Who Can Apply (Organizations) The program is open to a wide range of entities, including: For-profit organizations Non-profit organizations Provincial, territorial, and municipal governments Crown corporations Indigenous governments and organizations Agencies representing Indigenous communities Applicants must demonstrate strong connections with employers to ensure meaningful work placements for youth. Who Benefits (Youth Participants) Although organizations apply for funding, the ultimate beneficiaries are young Canadians , particularly: Recent graduates Individuals seeking digital skills training Youth entering the labor market Notably, post-secondary education is not required for participants in: Yukon Northwest Territories Nunavut This ensures inclusivity and reduces barriers to participation. Program Benefits The DS4Y program offers extensive advantages for both organizations and youth: For Organizations Access to federal funding Opportunity to lead national-level initiatives Strengthened partnerships with employers and communities For Youth Hands-on work experience Exposure to cutting-edge technologies Improved employability and career readiness Opportunities in high-demand digital sectors Government Vision and Strategic Importance According to Minister Mélanie Joly, the program reflects Canada’s commitment to preparing its youth for a rapidly changing economy driven by technology. The initiative aims to: Ensure long-term economic growth Promote innovation Build a resilient and adaptable workforce Deadline The deadline to submit a complete application is: 👉 May 22, 2026 Late applications will not be considered, so early preparation is strongly encouraged. How to Apply Interested organizations must submit their proposals through the official Digital Skills for Youth program platform managed by Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada. Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the program guidelines and ensure all required documentation is included. For more information click here Discover more opportunities here The post Canada’s $23.8M Digital Skills for Youth Program 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Consultant – Religious and Traditional Leaders Engagement (IOM Nigeria)
summary: Applications are now open for the Consultant – Religious and Traditional Leaders Engagement position in Lagos, Nigeria, offered by the International Organization for Migration . This consultancy role presents a unique opportunity for experienced professionals in migration governance, communication, and social behavior change to contribute to impactful projects addressing migration challenges in Nigeria. With a strong focus on collaboration with religious and traditional leaders, this role is central to strengthening migration governance, protection systems, and community engagement across multiple Nigerian states. About the Organization The International Organization for Migration (IOM), established in 1951, is a leading intergovernmental organization and a key partner within the United Nations system. IOM works globally to promote humane and orderly migration , providing support and guidance to governments and migrants alike. Through its programs, IOM focuses on: Migration governance Protection of vulnerable populations Reintegration and sustainable development Community-based engagement strategies Position Overview Role: Consultant – Religious and Traditional Leaders Engagement Location: Lagos Contract Type: Consultancy (up to 11 months) Initial Duration: 6 months Application Deadline: May 7, 2026 Job ID: 19887 This consultancy is part of IOM Nigeria’s Migration Management initiatives, supported by international partners including the Government of the Netherlands and the European Union. Project Context and Scope This role operates within major programs such as: COMPASS 2.0 (Cooperation for Migration Partnerships on Sustainable Solutions) EU-funded Support to Migration Governance Programme The consultancy aims to strengthen collaboration with religious and traditional leaders to: Promote safe and regular migration Support victims of trafficking (TiP/SoM) Improve referral systems and protection frameworks Drive behavior change at the community level The project spans multiple states, including: Lagos Edo Delta Kano Key Responsibilities The consultant will lead strategic and operational activities, including: 1. Network Strengthening Map and engage religious and traditional stakeholders Expand and institutionalize leader networks across four states Finalize and implement engagement roadmaps 2. Behavior Change Strategy Co-develop culturally sensitive communication tools Create sermon guides and IEC materials Promote safe migration messaging across faith communities 3. Referral and Protection Systems Establish and strengthen referral pathways Align faith actors with national referral mechanisms (NRM) Develop SOPs and protection frameworks 4. Stakeholder Engagement Facilitate national and state-level dialogues Coordinate community outreach initiatives Document commitments and outcomes 5. Knowledge Management Track sermon delivery and outreach impact Produce reports, case studies, and learning briefs Monitor community sentiment and engagement Deliverables and Performance Indicators The consultant will be evaluated based on key outputs: Inception report and workplan (30%) Network and behavior change toolkit development (40%) National and state dialogues (20%) Final reports and knowledge products (10%) Required Qualifications Education Advanced degree in: Communication studies Behavioral science Social sciences Migration management Experience Minimum 6 years in migration governance, protection, or SBC/C4D At least 4 years in strategic communication and advocacy Experience working with religious/traditional institutions Proven track record in developing behavior change tools Skills Strong analytical and communication skills Public speaking and facilitation expertise Proficiency in MS Office and data tools Ability to produce high-quality reports Languages Fluency in English (required) Knowledge of Nigerian languages (preferred) Core Competencies IOM seeks candidates who demonstrate: Integrity and professionalism Respect for diversity and inclusion Strong teamwork and collaboration Accountability and results-driven mindset Effective communication and leadership Benefits and Work Conditions While this is a consultancy role, selected candidates will benefit from: High-level international exposure Collaboration with UN systems and global partners Opportunity to influence migration policy and community outcomes Coverage under IOM’s occupational Compensation Plan Note: Consultants must provide their own health insurance for non-work-related conditions. Important Notes IOM maintains a zero-tolerance policy on misconduct, including harassment and discrimination No application fees are required at any stage Only applications submitted through the official IOM recruitment system will be considered Shortlisted candidates will be contacted How to Apply Applicants must submit their application through the official IOM e-Recruitment platform before the deadline. For more information click here APPLY HERE Discover more opportunities here The post Consultant – Religious and Traditional Leaders Engagement (IOM Nigeria) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Young Leaders Program 2027 in Africa: Launch Your Global Consulting Career with McKinsey in Nairobi & Addis Ababa
summary: Applications are now open for the highly competitive Young Leaders Program (YLP) 2027 , an exceptional two-year full-time consulting opportunity designed to develop Africa’s next generation of leaders. Hosted by McKinsey Company , this program offers ambitious graduates in Kenya and Ethiopia the chance to gain hands-on experience, solve real-world business challenges, and accelerate their professional growth in a globally recognized firm. Whether you are passionate about strategy, problem-solving, or driving meaningful impact across industries, this program is designed to equip you with the tools, mentorship, and exposure needed to thrive in consulting and beyond. About the Young Leaders Program (YLP) The Young Leaders Program is a two-year immersive consulting experience where selected fellows work directly with clients across various sectors. Participants are embedded in small teams, typically consisting of 3–5 consultants, and contribute actively to solving complex business and societal challenges across Africa. Fellows are based in either: Nairobi office Addis Ababa office In addition, there may be opportunities to collaborate with teams across other African offices, offering regional exposure and cross-cultural experience. What You Will Do (Your Impact) As a fellow, your role goes far beyond observation. You will be deeply involved in all phases of consulting projects, including: Gathering and analyzing critical data Formulating and testing hypotheses Developing strategic recommendations Presenting findings to client leadership Supporting implementation of solutions This hands-on involvement ensures that you build real consulting skills from day one while contributing to meaningful outcomes for clients. Program Structure and Career Progression The YLP is carefully structured to transform high-potential graduates into future leaders: Duration: 2 years (full-time) Role: Fellow (entry-level consulting position) Post-program opportunity: High-performing participants are promoted to a Business Analyst role , marking the next step in their consulting careers Training, Mentorship, and Learning Experience One of the most distinctive features of this program is its strong focus on professional development. Continuous Learning Participants benefit from a structured learning environment that combines: Formal training sessions On-the-job learning Regular performance feedback Mentorship and Coaching Each fellow is supported by: Experienced consultants A dedicated partner or senior mentor Team-based coaching throughout projects This mentorship model ensures rapid personal and professional growth. Global Exposure Working at McKinsey means joining a truly global network: Colleagues from 65+ countries Over 100 nationalities represented Opportunities to collaborate across regions Why You Should Apply (Key Benefits) Joining the Young Leaders Program comes with numerous advantages: Accelerated career growth in consulting Exposure to high-impact projects across industries Development of analytical and leadership skills Access to world-class mentorship Opportunity to work in an international environment Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits package Strong professional network across Africa and globally Eligibility Criteria To be considered for the 2027 cohort, applicants must meet the following criteria: Be a 2026 or early 2027 graduate Be based in Kenya or Ethiopia Hold an undergraduate or master’s degree with strong academic performance Have less than 1 year of continuous work experience Demonstrate leadership through extracurricular or professional activities Possess strong analytical and problem-solving skills Be fluent in English (and local language where applicable) Application Requirements Applicants must submit: A one-page CV Academic details including: University GPA or ranking High school grades Each application is linked to a specific job ID depending on location: Addis Ababa: Job ID 16433 APPLY HERE Nairobi: Job ID 15122 APPLY HERE Work Culture and Values The program is designed for individuals who thrive in high-performance environments. McKinsey values: Curiosity and initiative Resilience and adaptability Ethical leadership and integrity Collaboration and teamwork From day one, fellows are encouraged to contribute ideas, challenge assumptions, and make a tangible impact. Deadline No specific application deadline has been publicly stated. However, due to the competitive nature of the program, early application is strongly recommended . APPLY HERE Discover more opportunities here The post Young Leaders Program 2027 in Africa: Launch Your Global Consulting Career with McKinsey in Nairobi Addis Ababa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: CREATIVE Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Clinical Research Training Opportunity in Africa (EU-Funded Programme)
summary: The Clinical ResEArch Training fellowship with Industry involVEment (CREATIVE Fellowship) is a prestigious, fully funded international programme designed to strengthen clinical research capacity across Sub-Saharan Africa. Funded by the European Union through Global Health EDCTP3, this fellowship offers a rare opportunity for early-career researchers to gain advanced academic training, practical research experience, and exposure to the global health industry. This multi-country initiative is structured to empower African researchers by equipping them with the knowledge, skills, and networks necessary to contribute meaningfully to health research and innovation on the continent and beyond. Programme Overview The CREATIVE Fellowship runs for a duration of 30 months and provides a comprehensive, multi-dimensional training experience. Fellows will be immersed in a rigorous programme that combines: Academic advancement Practical clinical research Industry exposure Professional mentorship The fellowship is expected to begin in October 2026 and will be implemented across six African countries. Key Components of the Fellowship 1. Work Placement at Leading Research Institutions Fellows will be placed at one of six internationally recognized health research institutions in Africa. Each fellow will remain at their assigned institution throughout the fellowship, actively participating in impactful clinical research projects. The host institutions include: Haramaya University (Ethiopia) Kintampo Health Research Centre, Ghana Health Service (Ghana) Medical Research Council Unit, The Gambia MRC/UVRI and LSHTM Uganda Research Unit (Uganda) Zambart (Zambia) Biomedical Research and Training Institute (Zimbabwe) Each institution will host two fellows, making a total of 12 fellowship slots available . 2. Academic Training (Master’s Degree) Participants will enroll in a Distance Learning MSc programme at the London School of Hygiene Tropical Medicine (LSHTM), UK. Fellows will choose between: MSc in Clinical Trials (9 fellows) MSc in Epidemiology (3 fellows) The academic programme will be completed within the fellowship duration and is designed to complement the hands-on research experience. 3. Industry Exposure Fellows will have the opportunity to apply for a competitive industry internship , allowing them to gain valuable insights into how clinical research operates within the private and global health sectors. 4. Structured Mentorship A key strength of the CREATIVE Fellowship is its strong mentorship component. Fellows will receive guidance from experienced professionals who will support: MSc completion Career development Networking opportunities Transition into post-fellowship roles Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Be a national of Ethiopia, Ghana, The Gambia, Uganda, Zambia, or Zimbabwe Apply for a fellowship hosted in their country of nationality Hold a first- or second-class honours degree in a relevant field such as: Natural sciences Life sciences Epidemiology Statistics Medicine or related disciplines Have at least one year of experience in health research Meet Band B English language requirements (test results may be submitted later if not available at application stage) Be available to begin the fellowship full-time in October 2026 Provide two reference letters , including one from a current employer Application Requirements Applicants must complete an online application and upload the following documents: Curriculum Vitae (CV) Academic transcripts Academic certificates Two reference letters Proof of nationality Additionally, applicants must submit a personal statement addressing: Motivation for applying Academic and professional background Expected outcomes from the fellowship Career plans after completion Preferred MSc programme (optional preference) Applicants are encouraged to provide detailed and well-structured responses, as this plays a crucial role in the selection process. Selection and Shortlisting Criteria Applications will be evaluated based on: Academic merit (grades and qualifications) Relevant work experience Motivation and clarity of career goals Only the most competitive candidates will be shortlisted for interviews. Important Dates Application Deadline: 29 May 2026 Interview Period: July 2026 Programme Start: October 2026 Why You Should Apply The CREATIVE Fellowship is more than just a scholarship—it is a career-transforming opportunity that bridges academia, research, and industry. Fellows gain: A fully funded Master’s degree Real-world clinical research experience Access to global health networks Career advancement opportunities This programme is ideal for individuals passionate about advancing healthcare research and making a lasting impact in Africa. Click here to learn more about CREATIVE Fellowship 2026 To discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, click Here The post CREATIVE Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Clinical Research Training Opportunity in Africa (EU-Funded Programme) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: WTO Fish Fund Extends Deadline to 8 May 2026: Expanded Opportunity for Developing Nations to Access Up to USD 300,000 in Grants
summary: The World Trade Organization (WTO) Fish Fund has officially extended the deadline for its second Call for Proposals to 8 May 2026 , offering eligible countries additional time to submit applications for critical funding support. This extension provides a significant opportunity for developing and least-developed WTO member nations to access financial and technical assistance aimed at implementing the Agreement on Fisheries Subsidies. Overview of the WTO Fish Fund Call for Proposals Originally launched on 2 February 2026 , the second Call for Proposals invites applications from WTO members that meet specific eligibility criteria. The initiative is part of a broader global effort to promote sustainable fishing practices and ensure effective implementation of international fisheries agreements. The Fish Fund operates as a targeted support mechanism to assist countries in strengthening their capacity, improving governance, and aligning national policies with global fisheries standards. Purpose of the Fish Fund The WTO Fish Fund was established under Article 7 of the Agreement on Fisheries Subsidies with a clear mission: To provide technical assistance To support capacity-building initiatives To enable effective implementation of fisheries subsidy disciplines This initiative plays a crucial role in addressing harmful fishing subsidies that contribute to overfishing and environmental degradation. Types of Grants Available Applicants can apply for two distinct types of funding: 1. Project Preparation Grants Maximum funding: USD 50,000 Purpose: Conduct needs assessments Prepare implementation strategies Develop project proposals for future execution 2. Project Implementation Grants Maximum funding: USD 300,000 Purpose: Execute specific provisions of the Agreement Strengthen national systems and compliance mechanisms Support sustainable fisheries practices Previous Funding Outcomes The first Call for Proposals demonstrated strong global interest and impact: 26 project grants approved Total funding allocated: Nearly USD 3 million Approved in November 2025 by the Fish Fund Steering Committee These results highlight the fund’s growing importance in supporting fisheries governance worldwide. Eligibility Criteria To qualify for this funding opportunity: Applicants must be WTO members Must be classified as: Developing countries, or Least-developed countries (LDCs) Must have formally accepted the Agreement on Fisheries Subsidies by depositing their instrument of acceptance As of 26 March 2026 , the total number of WTO members that have accepted the Agreement has reached 119 , following recent acceptances by: Paraguay Samoa Saint Vincent and the Grenadines Institutional Partnerships The Fish Fund is hosted by the WTO and operates in collaboration with leading international organizations: Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO) International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD) World Bank Group These partnerships ensure that funded projects benefit from global expertise and technical support. Contributions and Funding Pool The Fish Fund continues to receive strong financial backing from member countries: Total contributions and commitments: CHF 15.7 million Equivalent to over USD 20 million Contributions from 18 WTO members , including: Australia Canada European Union France Germany Japan United Kingdom And several others This robust funding pool ensures sustainability and scalability of supported projects. Application Deadline All eligible WTO members must submit their proposals by: 8 May 2026 Late submissions will not be considered, making it essential for applicants to complete and submit their proposals before the deadline. How to Apply Applicants can access the official application portal to submit their proposals and find additional guidance on the process. Interested parties are encouraged to begin preparations early to ensure a strong and complete application. Why This Opportunity Matters The WTO Fish Fund represents a unique chance for developing nations to: Strengthen fisheries governance Promote environmental sustainability Access international funding and expertise Align with global trade and environmental standards With the deadline now extended, more countries have the opportunity to participate and benefit from this impactful initiative. For more information about this opportunity visit here. Discover more global grant opportunities here. title: Rainforest Investigations Network Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Investigative Journalists Worldwide
summary: The Pulitzer Center is now accepting applications for the sixth cohort of its prestigious Rainforest Investigations Network (RIN) Fellowship 2026 . This highly competitive, fully funded, yearlong program supports experienced investigative journalists working on critical environmental stories tied to deforestation, climate change, corruption, and governance across the world’s three major tropical rainforest regions: The Amazon The Congo Basin Southeast Asia Application Deadline: May 22, 2026 (11:59 PM EST) About the Rainforest Investigations Network (RIN) The Rainforest Investigations Network is a global initiative designed to strengthen cross-border investigative journalism focused on uncovering the root causes of rainforest destruction. The fellowship enables journalists to explore: Financial incentives behind deforestation Illegal activities and environmental crimes Global supply chains contributing to forest degradation Governance failures and corruption RIN Fellows collaborate across countries and newsrooms, producing impactful stories that reveal hidden systems driving environmental harm. What the Fellowship Offers Selected fellows will receive comprehensive support, including: Financial Support Coverage of salary (full or partial) depending on experience and location Funding for reporting expenses , including: Travel Research Hiring consultants Training Mentorship Editorial guidance from a multidisciplinary team Training in: Investigative techniques Data analysis Satellite imagery tools Corporate and financial investigations Global Collaboration Work alongside a network of international investigative journalists Opportunities to collaborate on cross-border investigations Access to data, documents, and exclusive reporting resources Outreach Engagement Support from engagement teams to: Share findings with communities Present work in schools and universities Reach global audiences What the Program is Looking For The RIN Fellowship seeks seasoned investigative journalists who: Have a strong track record in investigative reporting Focus on or are based in rainforest regions Can propose ambitious, original, and impactful investigations Are open to collaboration and teamwork Use innovative, data-driven approaches Priority Areas Special emphasis is placed on projects that: Take a cross-border investigative approach Examine systemic drivers of deforestation Investigate financial systems and global supply chains Transparency Governance Track Two fellowship slots are reserved for projects focusing on: Financial institutions enabling environmental destruction Corporate accountability Legal loopholes and governance failures Corruption linked to deforestation Examples of Past Investigations Previous fellows have worked on impactful stories such as: Mining linked to electric vehicle supply chains Corporate pollution in the Amazon Financial flows into agribusiness driving deforestation Carbon offset projects with questionable claims Government-backed illegal deforestation initiatives Fellowship Commitment Fellows are expected to: Work full-time for one year on their investigation Publish their stories during the fellowship period Participate in: Regular meetings Training sessions Peer collaboration They must also maintain transparency and actively contribute to the network’s collaborative environment. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Be experienced investigative journalists Work as staff reporters or freelancers (with a hosting newsroom) Have a strong understanding of environmental, political, and economic issues Be able to collaborate across borders and teams Commit to outreach and public engagement activities Journalists outside rainforest regions can apply but must focus their reporting on those regions. Application Requirements Applicants must submit: Statement of Purpose (500 words) Career goals and fit for the fellowship Collaboration potential Investigative Project Proposal (1,000 words) A detailed, original, and well-researched story idea Must demonstrate feasibility and impact Publication Audience Plan (500 words) Distribution strategy Target audience and engagement approach Three Published Investigations (from the past 3 years) Letter of Support from a newsroom Three Professional References Resume/CV Application Languages Applications are accepted in: English Portuguese Spanish French Bahasa Indonesia Ask Me Anything (AMA) Sessions Applicants can join live sessions with editors in multiple languages: English: April 28, 2026 Bahasa Indonesia: April 29, 2026 Spanish: April 30, 2026 Portuguese: May 5, 2026 French: May 7, 2026 How to Apply Submit your application via the official form before the deadline: Deadline: May 22, 2026 (11:59 PM EST) For inquiries: rin@pulitzercenter.org Why This Fellowship Matters The destruction of tropical rainforests is one of the most urgent global challenges. This fellowship empowers journalists to uncover hidden truths, expose powerful systems, and drive accountability on a global scale. For more information about this opportunity visit here. Discover more fellowship programs here. title: Data Governance for Inclusive Digital & AI Futures: Free Global Online Course by ITU, UNDP & UNESCO (2026)
summary: The rapid evolution of digital technologies and artificial intelligence has made data governance a critical priority for governments, institutions, and development practitioners worldwide. To address this need, leading international organizations have launched a comprehensive training program titled “Data Governance for Inclusive Digital AI Futures.” This course is designed to equip participants with the knowledge, frameworks, and practical tools needed to build inclusive, secure, and rights-based data systems. Offered as a fully funded training opportunity, this course provides a unique chance for professionals across the globe to strengthen their understanding of how data can drive equitable digital transformation. Overview of the Course This online instructor-led training program focuses on building foundational and practical expertise in data governance. It emphasizes the importance of creating trustworthy data ecosystems that support innovation while safeguarding rights and ensuring inclusivity. Key Details: Registration Period: 21 April 2026 – 31 May 2026 (Deadline) Course Dates: 23 June 2026 – 21 July 2026 Location: Global (Online) Duration: 12 hours Course Level: Intermediate Language: English Cost: Free of charge for selected applicants Training Type: Online instructor-led Organizers and Partners This course is jointly organized by globally recognized institutions working at the intersection of technology, development, and policy: International Telecommunication Union (ITU) United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) UNESCO The program is supported by the European Union’s Global Gateway initiative, ensuring accessibility for participants from diverse backgrounds. Course Description The course offers a deep dive into the principles and practices of data governance in the digital age. Participants will explore how to design and implement frameworks that enable: Secure and ethical data use Inclusive digital transformation Evidence-based policymaking The curriculum integrates insights from the Data Governance Toolkit , developed collaboratively by UNESCO, UNDP, ITU, and the African Union Commission under the Broadband Commission Working Group. What You Will Learn Participants will gain both theoretical understanding and practical insights, including: Policy Dimensions of Data Governance Designing regulatory frameworks Aligning with national digital strategies and SDGs Institutional Frameworks Governance structures for data management Stakeholder coordination and accountability Technical Foundations Data quality, interoperability, and transparency Building secure and trusted data ecosystems Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) Governance components of DPI Role of data in public service delivery AI and Data Ecosystems Importance of reliable data for AI systems Safeguards for ethical AI deployment Global Case Studies Country experiences and best practices Actionable strategies for implementation Learning Approach The course uses an interactive and practical methodology to enhance engagement and knowledge retention: Live instructor-led sessions Case studies and real-world examples Peer discussions and collaborative learning Practical tools for policymaking Target Audience This course is ideal for: Policymakers Government officials Digital transformation practitioners Data governance professionals Development sector experts It is particularly relevant for those involved in shaping digital policies, managing data systems, or working on AI-related initiatives. Tutors and Coordinators Participants will learn from experienced experts in the field: Tutors: Leona Verdadero, Christophe Ji Coordinators: Queenie Tam, Ghazi Mabrouk, Angel Draev Why You Should Apply This course offers several valuable benefits: Gain globally recognized expertise in data governance Learn directly from international organizations Access practical tools and frameworks for real-world application Understand the intersection of data, AI, and public policy Network with professionals from around the world Participate at no cost if selected Inclusivity and Accessibility The program strongly promotes diversity and inclusion. Applications are encouraged from: Women professionals Participants from developing countries Individuals from least developed countries, small island developing states, and landlocked developing countries The course is open to all professionals regardless of background, ensuring equal access to learning opportunities. How to Apply Interested applicants should complete their registration before the deadline: Deadline: 31 May 2026 Applicants may also refer to the official registration document for detailed instructions and requirements. Final Thoughts As data becomes the backbone of digital economies and AI systems, understanding how to govern it responsibly is no longer optional—it is essential. This course provides a timely opportunity to build expertise that can shape inclusive and sustainable digital futures. Visit here for more information and to apply Discover more global courses on OFY here. title: Comprehensive Security Fellowship in Bonn 2027/28 – Academic & Practitioner Tracks on International Politics, Strategic Resilience, and Global Security Challenges
summary: Comprehensive Security Fellowship Call 2027/28 – AIA NRW (Bonn, Germany) The Academy in Bonn (AIA NRW) has officially opened its Fellowship Call for the 2027/28 cohort , inviting applications from scholars and practitioners working in the broad field of international politics, security studies, and strategic resilience . This prestigious fellowship focuses on rethinking global security in an era marked by geopolitical uncertainty, technological transformation, and evolving international alliances. The program emphasizes the concept of “Comprehensive Security for a Changing World: Building Strategic and Technological Resilience” , reflecting Europe’s shifting security landscape shaped by open conflicts, global competition, and institutional challenges. About the Fellowship Program This fellowship is designed to bring together interdisciplinary researchers and professionals to explore how modern security frameworks can address interconnected global risks. Key focus areas include: International security and defence cooperation Governance frameworks in global politics Technological governance and digital transformation Supply chain security and economic resilience Protection of democratic institutions Strategic foresight and global risk analysis Emerging geopolitical and security threats The program supports innovative research and practice-oriented reflection that contributes to both academic discourse and real-world policy solutions . Fellowship Tracks Applicants can apply under one of two distinct tracks: 1. Academic Track This track is intended for candidates with a strong research background. It requires: A clear academic research focus within international politics or security studies Engagement with existing or emerging scholarly debates A defined contribution to academic knowledge and theory-building 2. Practitioner’s Track This track is designed for professionals working in relevant fields. It requires: Reflection on international politics from practical or policy experience Integration of real-world professional insights Contribution to applied understanding of global security challenges Fellowship Expectations and Responsibilities Selected fellows are expected to actively participate in the intellectual life of the Academy in Bonn. Responsibilities include: Full-time residence in Bonn during the fellowship period Regular participation in seminars, colloquia, and academic discussions Contribution to workshops and institutional events Engagement in publication activities and research outputs Willingness to support knowledge transfer and public engagement initiatives Optional involvement in social media and outreach activities Fellowship Period Duration: April 2027 – March 2028 Fellows must be present in Bonn throughout the program without extended absences Application Requirements Applicants must submit a single PDF file including the following documents: Mandatory Documents Letter of motivation Research/project proposal (maximum 5 pages), including: Abstract Research questions or guiding objectives State of research overview Detailed work plan and timeline Expected outputs Added value of conducting research at AIA NRW in Bonn Short CV (maximum 2 pages) Additional Requirements for Academic Track Applicants Certified PhD certificate Full list of academic publications 2–3 recent peer-reviewed journal articles (in English) Key Dates and Deadlines Application Deadline: 31 May 2026 Decision Announcement: Mid-September 2026 Fellowship Period: April 2027 – March 2028 Selection Process Applications will be reviewed by the Academic Board , which will evaluate submissions based on academic quality, relevance to the fellowship theme, and potential contribution to international political and security discourse. Additional Information Applicants are encouraged to: Demonstrate innovative thinking in addressing global security challenges Highlight interdisciplinary approaches Show relevance to contemporary geopolitical transformations Engage with policy, academic, or strategic communities Contact Information Dr. Katja Freistein Academic Coordinator, Fellowship Programme AIA NRW (Bonn, Germany) Apply here For more information about this opportunity visit here. Discover more fellowship programs here.
